get.summary: Changes the current company context for the API session to the specified company.
get.description:
This method attempts to retrieve the specified company by its ID. If the company is
            not already open in the session, it will attempt to open it. The response includes the company details
            serialized in JSON format. If the company does not exist, an error response is returned.

parameters.name: CompanyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the company to switch to.

get.summary: Retrieves the collection of plugin table names associated with the specified company.

parameters.name: CompanyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the company for which to retrieve plugin table names.

get.summary: Retrieves the most recent error message for the specified company and error code.

parameters.name: CompanyId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the company for which to retrieve the last error.

parameters.name: errorCode
parameters.description: The error code specifying the type of error to retrieve.

post.summary: Inserts a new entity of the specified type into the system.
post.description:
This method requires a valid authentication token. If the token is invalid or expired,
            the method returns a 401 Unauthorized response.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity type to insert. This must match a valid entity type recognized by the system.

requestBody.description:
A JSON object representing the entity to be inserted. The structure of the JSON must match the expected
            schema for the specified entity type.

post.summary: Inserts a list of entities into the specified data store.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity type to insert. This determines the target data store.

requestBody.description:
A JSON object containing the list of entities to be inserted. The structure of the JSON must match the
            expected format for the specified entity type.

post.summary: Inserts a master entity and its associated child entities into the system.
post.description:
This method deserializes the provided master and child entities, validates them, and
            attempts to insert them into the system. If the operation is successful, the method returns an !:ErrorCodes.Success response. If an error occurs, an appropriate error code is returned.

parameters.name: masterName
parameters.description: The name of the master entity type to be inserted.

parameters.name: childName
parameters.description: The name of the child entity type to be associated with the master entity.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the master entity and a collection of child entities to be inserted. The master entity
            is provided in the UnicontaWebAPI.Controllers.MasterDetailRequest.Master property, and the child entities are provided in
            the UnicontaWebAPI.Controllers.MasterDetailRequest.Details property.

post.summary:
Inserts a collection of child records associated with a specified master record using the provided master
and detail data.
post.description:
The method requires a valid authentication token. The master and child entity names
            must correspond to valid entity types recognized by the API. All child records will be linked to the
            specified master record before insertion.

parameters.name: masterName
parameters.description: The name of the master entity type to which the child records will be linked. Cannot be null or empty.

parameters.name: childName
parameters.description: The name of the child entity type to insert. Cannot be null or empty.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the serialized master entity and a collection of serialized child entities to be
            inserted. Must not be null.

delete.summary: Deletes an entity of the specified type from the system.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity type to delete. This must match the expected entity type in the system.

requestBody.description:
A JSON object representing the entity to be deleted. The structure of the JSON must match the expected
            format for the specified entity type.

delete.summary: Deletes a list of entities of the specified type.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity type to delete. This must match the entity type expected by the API.

requestBody.description:
A JSON object containing the list of entities to delete. The structure of the JSON must match the expected
            format for the specified entity type.

put.summary: Updates an entity of the specified type with the provided data.
put.description:
The method processes the provided JSON data, deserializes
            it into the appropriate entity type, and attempts to update the entity in the system.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity type to update. This must match a valid entity type recognized by the system.

requestBody.description:
A JSON object containing the updated data for the entity. The structure of the JSON must match the expected
            format for the specified entity type.

put.summary: Updates the specified fields of an entity based on the provided original and modified values.
put.description:
Ensure that the entityName corresponds to a valid
            entity type and that the  originalModifiedRequest contains valid data for both the
            original and modified entities.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity to update. This value determines the type of entity being processed.

requestBody.description: An object containing the original and modified versions of the entity.  The UnicontaWebAPI.Controllers.OriginalModifiedRequest.Original property represents the current state of the entity,  and the UnicontaWebAPI.Controllers.OriginalModifiedRequest.Modified property represents the desired updated state.

put.summary: Updates a list of entities of the specified type in the system.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity type to update. This must match a valid entity type in the system.

requestBody.description:
A JSON object containing the list of entities to update. The structure of the JSON must match the expected
            format for the specified entity type.

put.summary: Updates a list of modified entities in the system based on the provided original and modified data.
put.description:
This method compares the original and modified lists of entities and updates the system
            with the changes.  The caller must ensure that the entityName matches a valid entity type
            and that the lists in  originalModifiedListRequest are properly serialized representations
            of the entities.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity type being updated. This must match the entity type in the system.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the original and modified lists of entities.  The original list represents the current
            state of the entities, while the modified list contains the updated values.

post.summary: Validates the journal request by checking its header and lines, and processes it using the posting API.

requestBody.description:
The journal request containing the header and lines to be validated and processed.  The header and lines
            must not be null.

post.summary: Posts a journal entry to the system.

requestBody.description:
The journal request containing the header and lines to be posted. The header provides metadata for the
            journal,  and the lines represent individual transactions or entries within the journal.

post.summary: Posts a daily journal to the general ledger system.

requestBody.description:
The request containing the details of the daily journal to be posted, including the journal name,  posting
            date, comment, and journal lines. This parameter cannot be null, and the `Journal`  property must not
            be empty.

post.summary: Posts an inventory journal to the system.
post.description:
The method processes the provided JSON data, deserializes
            it into the appropriate entity type, and attempts to update the entity in the system.

requestBody.description:
The inventory journal request containing the journal details, lines, and additional parameters required for
            posting. This parameter must not be null, and the UnicontaWebAPI.Controllers.InvJournalRequest.Journal property must not
            be null or empty.

post.summary:
Generates a debtor invoice based on the provided parameters and returns the resulting PDF file or an error
message.
post.description:
This method validates the provided parameters, retrieves the debtor order and order
            lines if necessary, and posts the invoice using the provided API. If the operation is successful, the
            generated invoice is returned as a PDF file. Otherwise, an appropriate error message is returned.

requestBody.description: The parameters required to generate the invoice, including the debtor order and associated order lines.

post.summary:
Generates a creditor invoice based on the provided request parameters and returns the resulting PDF file or
an error message.
post.description:
This method validates the input parameters, retrieves the necessary order and line
            details if not provided,  and posts the invoice using the provided API. If the operation is successful, a
            PDF file of the invoice is returned.  Otherwise, an appropriate error message is returned.

requestBody.description:
The parameters required to generate the creditor invoice, including the order and its associated lines.  The
            request must not be null, and it must contain a valid order and at least one line item.

get.summary: Retrieves the PDF representation of an invoice identified by the specified GUID.
get.description:
This method requires the user to be authenticated. If the authentication token is
            missing or invalid,  the method will return a response prompting the user to log in again.  If the provided
            GUID is invalid or the invoice cannot be retrieved, an error response will be returned.

parameters.name: GuidString
parameters.description: A string representation of the GUID that uniquely identifies the invoice.  Must be a valid GUID format.

get.summary: Retrieves a PDF representation of a debtor invoice based on the specified account, date, and invoice number.
get.description:
This method requires the user to be authenticated and authorized. If the authentication
            token is missing or invalid,  the method will return a message prompting the user to log in again.

parameters.name: Account
parameters.description: The account identifier associated with the invoice. This value cannot be null or empty.

parameters.name: Date
parameters.description: The date of the invoice. This value must represent a valid date.

parameters.name: InvoiceNumber
parameters.description: The unique number identifying the invoice. This value must be a positive integer.

post.summary: Creates production lines for a specified production order.
post.description:
Ensure that the CreateProductionLinesRequest contains a valid order
            number and other required details. If the order number is missing or invalid, the method will return an
            error message.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the details required to create production lines, including the order number,
            storage register, overwrite flag, and production time.

post.summary: Marks a production order as finished by reporting its completion details.
post.description:
Ensure that the ReportAsFinishedRequest object contains a valid order number and
            other  required fields before calling this method.

requestBody.description:
An object containing the details of the production order to be marked as finished, including the order
            number,  completion date, and additional metadata such as comments and transaction type.

post.summary: Validates and checks the status of a journal entry based on the provided request data.

requestBody.description:
The request containing the journal entry details to be checked, including the journal data, date, and
            simulation flag.

post.summary: Posts a daily journal entry to the system.

requestBody.description:
The request object containing the journal entry details, including the journal name, date, comment, and
            associated project name.

post.summary: Processes and posts an employee journal to the system.

requestBody.description: The request object containing the employee identifier, journal lines, posting date, comment, and other options.
